story.text: bdo, 2026-06-18, watching me spend a branch, an atom, a value-gate summons and a spawned judge to change ten English phrases ('on his phone' -> 'wherever he is'): 'The fact you're doing all this, to just edit the semantic of it so it's less pedantic, is poor. I want a backdoor route for quick edit ... to edit semantic and pedantic phrases, not syntax or schema' — packaged like whiteout and policy. As the loop, I want a proven low-impact door so a pedantic prose edit lands without the full work-particle mantra, while a change that touches real syntax or schema is refused at the door and routed back to the pipeline. The door is safe because it PROVES the edit is prose-only (the whiteout/carbon-copy shape) and the off-log gate re-runs the same proof server-side, so code can never slip through the prose door.

briefing.headline: the phrasing backdoor: pedantic prose edits land without the atom mantra
briefing.value: A session edits prose (a skill's wording, a docstring, an epic horizon), runs one route command, and it lands with no atom, no judge, no ceremony — because the change is PROVEN prose-only. The full pipeline (atom + second-set-of-eyes) is reserved for work that changes what the machine does; a phrase it never branches on is not a work-particle. bdo's exact ask, packaged like whiteout (done-line 0064): a session-usable move with computable teeth that refuses the instant it leaves its low-impact bounds.
briefing.why_now: bdo named it directly after watching the over-ceremony: 'a backdoor route for quick edit ... semantic and pedantic phrases, not syntax or schema.' The over-ceremony was real (a wording fix cost a branch + atom + summons + spawned judge), and the off-log gate (done-line 0068) forces an atom on every PR with no exemption for prose.
briefing.if_accepted: loop/phrasing.py proves any .md/.py/.json edit prose-only or names why not; pr.py phrasing marks a proven edit with one log admission; the off-log gate (client pen + server CI, one shared check) exempts a phrasing-clean branch from needing an atom. The next pedantic fix is: edit, `pr.py phrasing`, commit, `pr.py create` — and it sails.
briefing.if_rejected: Every prose correction keeps paying the full work-particle cost — a branch, an atom, a second pair of eyes — for a change the machine never reads as logic, and sessions keep over-engineering wording fixes (or avoid making them).
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Fail-safe and bounded: the door only ever WIDENS what lands without an atom, and only for changes proven not to touch code or schema; the same proof is re-run server-side, so a mislabelled change cannot pass. The default (no proof) is still the atom path. Reverting is deleting loop/phrasing.py and the orphan_reason branch; nothing depends on it. The teeth are tested (a code token, a renamed key, a changed verdict each refuse).
briefing.mechanism: loop/phrasing.py (pure, stdlib): phrasing_only(path, before, after) per kind — .md (body free; frontmatter keys + name/version protected), .py (tokenized; only COMMENT/STRING content may differ), .json (identical structure + non-prose values; only PROSE_KEYS string values may change) — and branch_phrasing_clean over a branch's non-log changes. loop/pr_audit.orphan_reason gains phrasing_clean as a SECOND way to be backed. pr.py: _range_atom_facts gathers+re-verifies phrasing_clean (the git reach), cmd_audit passes it, cmd_phrasing is the route (mark with a `phrasing` admission, refuse non-prose). tests/test_phrasing.py is the §10 teeth: prose across all three kinds passes and the gate calls the branch backed with no atom; a code token / renamed key / changed verdict each refuse and the gate still orphans the branch. Done-line 0117; full suite green.
